faq.question: What is the main takeaway from “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows”?
faq.answer: New construction windows replace the full framed opening, while replacement windows fit an existing opening. Compare costs, scope and the best use cases.

faq.question: How should a Bay Area homeowner apply the advice in “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows”?
faq.answer: Use the guidance in “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows” as a starting point, then account for the home's age, openings, exposure, existing damage, comfort goals, and budget before selecting a solution.

faq.question: Does “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows” apply to every window or door project?
faq.answer: No. The considerations in “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows” can change with frame material, product condition, installation method, local code, and the surrounding wall or waterproofing.

faq.question: When should I request professional help after reading “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows”?
faq.answer: Request an evaluation when the issue described in “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows” involves safety, water intrusion, damaged framing, uncertain measurements, code requirements, or a full replacement decision.
